text: Migrate every route and file from the Pages Router to the Next.js App Router. When finished, remove the pages dir entirely. Ensure the proper App Router APIs are used. If a Pages Router API was used that no longer exists in App Router, replace it with the newer version or the new pattern. Make sure to add types.

I’m going to map the current Next.js structure first, then migrate route-by-route into `app/` and replace any Pages Router-only APIs with App Router equivalents while keeping types in place. After that I’ll remove `pages/` and run a validation pass for route/API issues.

text: I’ve got the route behavior now. The key App Router work is: replace `getServerSideProps`/`getStaticProps`/`getStaticPaths` with server components and route params, replace `next/head` and `next/router`, and move API routes to `app/api/**/route.ts` with `Request`/`Response` plus typed payloads. I’m checking the shared styling and any hidden files before editing so I don’t miss an import that still points at `pages/`.

text: I’ve finished the inventory. The actual edits are going to be a full swap: add `app/layout.tsx`, `app/page.tsx`, `app/blog/page.tsx`, `app/blog/[id]/page.tsx`, App Router route handlers under `app/api/`, plus `not-found.tsx`, `error.tsx`, and `global-error.tsx`; convert `AppProvider` to a typed client component; then delete `pages/` outright and tighten the CSS for the new link-as-button pattern.

The first pass is in place. I’m doing a correctness sweep now for App Router-specific pitfalls: client components accidentally exporting metadata, method handlers that should not exist, and any type mismatches around `params`/layout props before I run a build.

The App Router files are all in place now. I’m verifying there are no leftover `pages/` references and then I’ll build the project so any Next-specific typing or route-handler issues surface immediately instead of being left latent.

text: The production build is in flight. I’m waiting for the type-check and route compilation stage, since that’s where App Router migrations usually fail if the `params` or route-handler signatures are off.

The build surfaced one concrete App Router change: `headers()` is async in this Next version, so the homepage needs to `await` it. I’m fixing that now and then re-running the build to see if any further type changes remain.
The homepage fix is in. I’m waiting on the second build pass now; if the remaining failures are type-related, they should point directly at the next App Router incompatibility.

text: The final verification run is in progress, and the directory check already came back clean. I’m waiting for the build tail so I can confirm the result after the cleanup step as well.

Migration complete.

- The app is now fully on the App Router in [`/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx`](sandbox:/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx), [`/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx`](sandbox:/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx), [`/home/user/workspace/app/blog/page.tsx`](sandbox:/home/user/workspace/app/blog/page.tsx), and [`/home/user/workspace/app/blog/[id]/page.tsx`](sandbox:/home/user/workspace/app/blog/[id]/page.tsx).
- The API routes moved to [`/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/route.ts`](sandbox:/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/route.ts) and [`/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts`](sandbox:/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts).
- Pages Router-only files were replaced with App Router equivalents: [`/home/user/workspace/app/not-found.tsx`](sandbox:/home/user/workspace/app/not-found.tsx), [`/home/user/workspace/app/error.tsx`](sandbox:/home/user/workspace/app/error.tsx), and [`/home/user/workspace/app/global-error.tsx`](sandbox:/home/user/workspace/app/global-error.tsx).
- The provider was converted to a typed client component in [`/home/user/workspace/components/AppProvider.tsx`](sandbox:/home/user/workspace/components/AppProvider.tsx), and the old JS version was removed.
- Shared styling was updated in [`/home/user/workspace/styles/globals.css`](sandbox:/home/user/workspace/styles/globals.css) for App Router layout structure and link-as-button navigation.
- The `pages/` directory has been removed entirely.

Validation:
- `npm run build` passes successfully.

One framework-driven change also landed in [`/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json`](sandbox:/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json): Next added `.next/dev/types/**/*.ts` to `include` during the build, and I kept it.

If you want, I can also do a second pass to simplify the remaining sample blog/API code further or add tests for the new route handlers.
